sentences.english: In some countries there's another way of keeping the cold out — going for a sauna.
sentences.korean: 일부 나라에서는 추위를 막는 또 다른 방법이 있어요 — 사우나를 가는 거예요.

sentences.english: Yes, Nordic countries like Sweden and Finland have a long history of taking saunas, but now they're popping up in Britain too.
sentences.korean: 네, 스웨덴과 핀란드 같은 북유럽 국가들은 사우나의 오랜 역사가 있는데, 지금은 영국에서도 생겨나고 있어요.

sentences.english: Have you ever been for a sauna, Georgie?
sentences.korean: 사우나 가본 적 있어요, 조지?

sentences.english: I have, yes. And if I'm honest, I'm not a huge fan.
sentences.korean: 가봤어요. 솔직히 엄청 좋아하진 않아요.

sentences.english: I don't really like being too hot.
sentences.korean: 너무 더운 게 별로예요.

quiz.question: What does '관찰적 데이터 — 자연적 행동을 관찰하여 수집한 정보' mean in English?
options: turn your nose up at something

sentences.english: It's uncomfortable.
sentences.korean: 불편하거든요.

sentences.english: What about you, Neil?
sentences.korean: 닐은요?

sentences.english: Oh really? I quite like a sauna.
sentences.korean: 오 정말? 나는 사우나 꽤 좋아해.

sentences.english: You feel really refreshed afterwards.
sentences.korean: 끝나고 나면 정말 상쾌해져.

sentences.english: A typical sauna is a small wooden room heated with steam to around 80°C.
sentences.korean: 전형적인 사우나는 증기로 약 80도까지 데운 작은 나무 방이에요.

quiz.question: What does '(관용구) ~을 하찮게 여겨 거부하다' mean in English?
options: turn your nose up at something
not all it's cracked up to be

sentences.english: It's hot enough to make anyone sweat, but are saunas good for your health as well?
sentences.korean: 누구든 땀을 흘릴 만큼 뜨거운데, 건강에도 좋을까요?

sentences.english: It's just so relaxing.
sentences.korean: 정말 편안해요.

sentences.english: It just makes you feel at ease.
sentences.korean: 그냥 편하게 느끼게 해줘요.

sentences.english: It's also great for relaxing muscles if you're tense or anything like that at all.
sentences.korean: 긴장해 있거나 할 때 근육 풀어주는 데도 정말 좋아요.

sentences.english: I always love coming down here when I'm a bit stressed out, and by the time that I'm finished, I feel like I've completely reset.
sentences.korean: 좀 스트레스받을 때 여기 오는 걸 항상 좋아해요.

quiz.question: What does '긴장한, 걱정되어 쉴 수 없는' mean in English?
options: turn your nose up at something
not all it's cracked up to be

sentences.english: It's wonderful.
sentences.korean: 끝나면 완전히 리셋된 기분이에요. 정말 멋져요.

sentences.english: The first speaker says saunas make her feel at ease — a phrase meaning comfortable and relaxed.
sentences.korean: 첫 번째 화자는 사우나가 편안하게 해준다고 해요 — at ease는 '편안하고 여유로운'이라는 뜻이에요.

sentences.english: And the next speaker thinks saunas are great if you feel tense — worried and unable to relax.
sentences.korean: 다음 화자는 긴장되면 사우나가 좋다고 해요 — tense는 '걱정되고 쉴 수 없는'이라는 뜻이에요.

sentences.english: I looked at the research really closely.
sentences.korean: 연구를 정말 면밀히 살펴봤어요.

sentences.english: I was fascinated by the evidence — huge benefits.
sentences.korean: 증거에 매료됐죠 — 엄청난 혜택이에요.

quiz.question: What does '쉬고 나서 다시 시작하여 기분이 나아지다' mean in English?
options: not all it's cracked up to be

sentences.english: Five million people in Finland — 90% of them use saunas.
sentences.korean: 핀란드 500만 명 중 90%가 사우나를 사용하고, 사우나 이용자에게 모든 원인 사망률이 40% 감소했어요.

sentences.english: There's a 40% reduction in all-cause mortality with the sauna-goers.
sentences.korean: / 사우나 이용자에게 40% 감소가 있다 모든 원인 사망률의.

sentences.english: This is what we would call observational data.
sentences.korean: 이것은 관찰적 데이터라고 부르는 거예요.

sentences.english: But we can't turn our noses up at the current data.
sentences.korean: 하지만 현재 데이터를 무시할 수는 없어요.

sentences.english: Damian discusses the evidence from Finland — a 40% reduction in deaths from all causes.
sentences.korean: 데이미언은 핀란드의 증거를 논의해요 — 모든 원인에서 사망이 40% 감소했어요.

quiz.question: What does '(관용구) ~을 하찮게 여겨 거부하다' mean in English?
options: turn your nose up at something
not all it's cracked up to be

sentences.english: But it's observational data — information from watching people's natural behaviour, not controlled trials.
sentences.korean: 하지만 이건 관찰적 데이터예요 — 통제된 시험이 아닌, 사람들의 자연적 행동을 관찰한 정보죠.

sentences.english: I asked you what the Finnish word sauna means.
sentences.korean: 핀란드어 '사우나'가 무슨 뜻인지 물었죠.

sentences.english: Is it: a) sweat, b) hot water, or c) bathhouse?
sentences.korean: a) 땀, b) 뜨거운 물, c) 목욕탕?

sentences.english: And I said bathhouse.
sentences.korean: 저는 목욕탕이라고 했어요.

sentences.english: Am I right?
sentences.korean: 맞나요?

sentences.english: You are absolutely correct.
sentences.korean: 완전히 맞아요.

sentences.english: Well done!
sentences.korean: 잘했어요!

quiz.question: What does '(관용구) ~을 하찮게 여겨 거부하다' mean in English?
options: not all it's cracked up to be
turn your nose up at something
